published_prior_art.cite: Hahn1998Econometrica + HiranoImbensRidder2003Econometrica + ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML: canonical ATE/AIPW efficiency baseline

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 ExactID empty; eid_dml_orth_uniform_v2 targeted weak-overlap studentization, not CEINR Gram alignment

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO gives LRO/FSIF GMM construction but not this ATE DML/ADML equivalence classifier

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: BrunsSmithDukesFellerOgburn2024ABW is cited as a 2024 JRSSB advance article, but the verified Oxford JRSSB citation is published 24 April 2025 with DOI qkaf019.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The Gram certificate controls only the residual loading alpha_J in L2(nu_P), but first-order influence-function equivalence also needs the nonresidual ATE component of the aggregated LRO moment pinned to m1(X)-m0(X)-theta0.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a third defect or explicit normalization assumption forcing kappa_J^T g_J's nonresidual component to equal the ATE plug-in component, so Xi_J decides full influence-function equivalence rather than residual-loading equivalence only.
Correct the Bruns-Smith et al. bibliography to the verified 2025 JRSSB publication metadata while noting the 2024 acceptance/arXiv history only if needed.

For Conjecture 2, write an explicit candidate g_2 with expectation zero, theta derivative A_2=1/2, and the displayed Gateaux derivative, instead of leaving the CEINR generation route at the level of a centered-contrast sketch.

recommended_next_step: Patch the full-IF normalization gap and bibliography, then rerun Stage -0.5 on the same CEINR Gram-alignment kernel.
